stories.headline: Trump on rampage at Nato summit: what does it mean for Ukraine? – The Latest
stories.summary: The US president is in combative mood as Nato leaders meet for a two-day summit in Ankara. There are divisions over Russia’s war in Ukraine, defence spending, and the US-Israel war in Iran with signs of the fragile ceasefire collapsing.The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, is holding talks with leaders as he rallies the European cause against Russia’s war that has reached the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, as Nato allies present an increasingly

stories.headline: In shift, Trump praises Zelensky, will let Ukraine build Patriot missiles
stories.summary: It was a dramatic departure from Trump’s more acerbic tone toward Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, whom he once derided as ungrateful.

stories.headline: Trump gives Zelenskyy vague promise of licence to manufacture Patriot missiles
stories.summary: Licence would be diplomatic coup for Kyiv but process of making munitions would likely be expensive, complex and longDonald Trump has told Volodymyr Zelenskyy that Ukraine may be allowed to manufacture Patriot missile interceptors to counter Russian ballistic attacks. stories.headline: Trump grants Ukraine licence to produce Patriot missiles
stories.summary: US president agrees to longstanding demand from Kyiv to make its own PAC-3 interceptors

stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire is 'over' after US and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: Speaking ahead of a Nato summit, the president says US negotiators can keep talking with the Iranians "if they want".

stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is ‘over’ and warns of further strikes

stories.headline: US to give Ukraine licence to produce Patriot air-defence interceptors, Trump says
stories.summary: Donald Trump said the US will grant Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot interceptors after more than six months of lobbying from Kyiv.

stories.headline: Trump threatens Spain trade, demands US take over Greenland at NATO summit
stories.summary: The US president described Spain as a 'terrible partner' over its actions during the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.headline: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

stories.headline: Trump takes aim at 'wasted cause' Spain and revives Greenland claim at Nato summit
stories.summary: The US president lashes out at fellow Nato member Spain, repeating an earlier threat to cut off trade relations.

stories.headline: EM Assets Slide as Trump Threatens Iran Strikes, New Blockade
stories.summary: Emerging-market assets sank Wednesday after the US renewed strikes on Iran and threatened to reimpose a blockade on its oil, pushing the price of crude further up and undermining the fragile ceasefire.

stories.headline: Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes
stories.summary: Leslie Vinjamuri, the President and CEO of the Chicago Council on Global Affairs joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why President Trump's latest Iran threats are aimed at gaining negotiating leverage rather than launching a full-scale war. stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iran and Reimposes Sanctions After Tanker Attacks
stories.summary: The military operation came hours after the U.S. Treasury revoked a waiver allowing global sales of Iranian oil.

stories.headline: US Stocks Slide as Oil Jumps After Trump Calls End of Iran Truce
stories.summary: US equities fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.

stories.headline: Trump tempers fury with allies and ends 'tremendously successful' NATO summit on high note
stories.summary: European leaders showcased their surging defence spending at the summit as they looked to convince Trump they're making good on a pledge to ramp up budgets and take more responsibility for their own security.

stories.headline: 'We're gonna hit 'em hard tonight,' Trump says about further air strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Trump had said that the ceasefire with Iran is "over" after Washington launched strikes on the country overnight, to which Iran responded by targeting US bases in the Gulf.

stories.headline: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
stories.summary: Although the Patriots are highly effective against Russia's ballistic missiles, they also have lengthy production times.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: If Platner Exits Senate Race, the Maine Democratic Party Chair Could Help Pick the Nominee
stories.summary: Charles Dingman, chair of the Maine Democratic Party and a progressive, would play a key role in choosing the state’s Democratic Senate candidate if Graham Platner leaves the race.

stories.headline: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race
stories.summary: Mr. Platner has not yet withdrawn from the race after a rape allegation, but Democrats in the state and nationally are already exploring potential alternatives.

stories.headline: U.S. and Iran launch fresh strikes following attacks on commercial ships
stories.summary: Iran said it had targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, after the U.S. launched strikes in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Democratic factions vie to pick Platner replacement before he leaves Maine race
stories.summary: Potential candidates and their backers moved quickly to gain an edge in the contest over who would replace Graham Platner if he exits the key Senate race.

stories.headline: Pakistan military says 42 killed in fighter attacks in Balochistan

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.headline: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Jitters Spur Rally in Oil
stories.summary:
A flare-up in geopolitical risks drove stocks lower, bond yields rose and oil climbed as President Donald Trump said a ceasefire with Iran may be over, noting the US would probably launch further strikes.
From New York to London and Tokyo, equities retreated. Almost 400 shares in the S&P 500 dropped, though chipmakers bounced.
Sarah Hunt, Chief Market Strategist at Alpine Saxon Woods, discusses these market gyrations amid geopolitical swings.

stories.headline: Estonia Willing to Contribute to Free Passage in Hormuz, PM Says
stories.summary: Estonia's Prime Minister Kristen Michal said the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, has been a positive one. He also said his country would be willing to contribute to efforts securing free passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Michal spoke to Bloomberg's Oliver Crook on the sidelines of the summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: In a country with more sand than soil, forging food security is not so straightforward. So, what does it take to grow resilience closer to home? Qatar in Motion explores how Qatar rewrote the rules of agriculture to reshape what is possible in the desert.

stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s attempts to revive oil flows from its reopened Ras Tanura terminal on the Persian Gulf are running into caution from customers, who are reluctant to load there because of renewed threats to shipping, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Judge Hannah C. Dugan Faces Fine But No Prison Time
stories.summary: The former Wisconsin judge, Hannah C. Dugan, was accused of ushering an undocumented immigrant out a side door as federal agents waited for him near her Milwaukee courtroom.

stories.headline: Premier League clubs' pre-tax losses surged more than 600% in a single season
stories.summary: English top-flight football clubs saw their combined pre-tax losses balloon from £135 million (€158mn) to £948 million (€1.1bn) in the 2024/25 season, even as their revenues climbed to a record high.

stories.summary: Oil jumped above $80 a barrel after US President Donald Trump said a tentative ceasefire with Iran was over, reigniting geopolitical risk premium in prices and triggering investor concern about supply disruptions.

stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.
